content.problemStatement:
Given a sequence XXX of length MMM, f(X)f(X)f(X) is defined as f(X)=в€‘i=1Mв€’1в€ЈXi+1в€’Xiв€Јf(X) = \sum_{i = 1}^{M - 1} |X_{i + 1} - X_i|f(X)=в€‘i=1Mв€’1вЂ‹в€ЈXi+1вЂ‹в€’XiвЂ‹в€Ј.

Specifically, if M=1M = 1M=1, then f(X)=0f(X) = 0f(X)=0.

For e.g., f([3,1,7,2])=в€Ј1в€’3в€Ј+в€Ј7в€’1в€Ј+в€Ј2в€’7в€Ј=13f([3, 1, 7, 2]) = |1-3| + |7-1| + |2-7| = 13f([3,1,7,2])=в€Ј1в€’3в€Ј+в€Ј7в€’1в€Ј+в€Ј2в€’7в€Ј=13

JJ has an array AAA of length NNN. He wants to divide the array AAA into two subsequences PPP and QQQ (possibly empty) such that the value of f(P)+f(Q)f(P) + f(Q)f(P)+f(Q) is as large as possible. (Note that each AiA_iAiвЂ‹ must belong to either subsequence PPP or subsequence QQQ).

Help him find this maximum value of f(P)+f(Q)f(P) + f(Q)f(P)+f(Q).

Note: A sequence XXX is a subsequence of a sequence YYY if XXX can be obtained from YYY by deletion of several (possibly, zero or all) elements.


Input Format

The first line contains TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the size of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,вЂ¦,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1вЂ‹,A2вЂ‹,вЂ¦,ANвЂ‹ denoting the array AAA.


Output Format
Output the largest value of f(P)+f(Q)f(P) + f(Q)f(P)+f(Q).
constraints: It is guaranteed that the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 300030003000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case-1: One way to divide the array into two subsequences PPP and QQQ as follows:
P=[1,6,2]P = [1, 6, 2]P=[1,6,2], Q=[4,3,4]Q = [4, 3, 4]Q=[4,3,4].
f(P)=в€Ј6в€’1в€Ј+в€Ј2в€’6в€Ј=9f(P) = |6 - 1| + |2 - 6| = 9f(P)=в€Ј6в€’1в€Ј+в€Ј2в€’6в€Ј=9 and f(Q)=в€Ј3в€’4в€Ј+в€Ј4в€’3в€Ј=2f(Q) = |3 - 4| + |4 - 3| = 2f(Q)=в€Ј3в€’4в€Ј+в€Ј4в€’3в€Ј=2.
Therefore f(P)+f(Q)=11f(P) + f(Q) = 11f(P)+f(Q)=11.
It can be proven that this is the maximum value of f(P)+f(Q)f(P) + f(Q)f(P)+f(Q).
Test case-2: One way to divide the array into two subsequences PPP and QQQ as follows:
P=[1,4]P = [1, 4]P=[1,4], Q=[2,3,5]Q = [2, 3, 5]Q=[2,3,5].
f(P)=в€Ј4в€’1в€Ј=3f(P) = |4 - 1|= 3f(P)=в€Ј4в€’1в€Ј=3 and f(Q)=в€Ј3в€’2в€Ј+в€Ј5в€’3в€Ј=3f(Q) = |3 - 2| + |5 - 3| = 3f(Q)=в€Ј3в€’2в€Ј+в€Ј5в€’3в€Ј=3.
Therefore f(P)+f(Q)=6f(P) + f(Q) = 6f(P)+f(Q)=6.
It can be proven that this is the maximum value of f(P)+f(Q)f(P) + f(Q)f(P)+f(Q).
Test case-3: One way to divide the array into two subsequences PPP and QQQ as follows:
P=[1,10]P = [1, 10]P=[1,10], Q=[]Q = []Q=[].
f(P)=в€Ј10в€’1в€Ј=9f(P) = |10 - 1| = 9f(P)=в€Ј10в€’1в€Ј=9 and f(Q)=0f(Q) = 0f(Q)=0.
Therefore f(P)+f(Q)=9f(P) + f(Q) = 9f(P)+f(Q)=9.
It can be proven that this is the maximum value of f(P)+f(Q)f(P) + f(Q)f(P)+f(Q).
